scenario: Lobsters work session - audit the Story contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: The answer provides zero orientation: no identification of Lobsters as a link-aggregator, no description of app layout (app/models, app/controllers, app/mailboxes, lib/extras), and no location of the Story model. An agent reading this would still need a complete fresh exploration to even know what project this is.

specificity.rationale: The answer cites concrete file:line references (app/models/vote.rb:181, :177, :185-191, :150-166), but every cited path is about voting mechanics — none name story.rb, any concern, or the associated models (comments, taggings, votes) the rubric requires.

justification.rationale: The answer explains why vote dispatch is polymorphic, which is irrelevant to this step; it offers no explanation of why Story is the central model or why the architecture hangs other records off it.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty is flagged about how project layers connect, because the answer never attempts to describe those layers at all.

relationship.rationale: The only relationship mentioned is the polymorphic vote-to-Story link via Vote#target — one incidental path that names Story as a lookup target, not as the central submission model with comments/votes/tags/flags hanging off it through associations or concerns.

An agent still needs a fresh exploration to find any of those contract pieces.
